title: PLUG Q1 Deep Dive: Revenue Growth Accelerates, Margin Progress Remains Focus
text: Plug Power exceeded Wall Street's expectations in Q1 2026, delivering revenue of $163.5 million versus analyst estimates of $141.1 million—a 15.9% beat. The company achieved 22.3% year-over-year growth. However, the adjusted EPS of -$0.18 missed expectations of -$0.10, while adjusted EBITDA reached -$84.5 million with a margin of -51.7%. Management credited robust performance in material handling, electrolyzer, and hydrogen fuel businesses alongside cost improvements. The electrolyzer segment demonstrated particularly strong momentum, with revenue jumping from $9.2 million to $40.8 million year-over-year. The company achieved 42 percentage point year-over-year improvement in gross margin through production cost reductions and operational efficiencies. Material handling benefited from customer engagement with major accounts like Amazon and Walmart, supported by the reinstated investment tax credit. Fuel logistics improved significantly, with margins rising 54 percentage points year-over-year through new third-party supply agreements and optimized hydrogen sourcing. Leadership emphasized achieving positive EBITDA by year-end through continued margin expansion and disciplined cash management. The company is pursuing an $8 billion opportunity funnel for electrolyzers and targeting new projects in aviation and synthetic fuels. Management expects sequential improvement in cash usage across the balance of the year supported by asset monetization and working capital optimization.
